Title: How to Get Better at American Football Introduction: American football is a complex sport that requires physical prowess, strategic thinking, and mental toughness. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced player looking to improve your skills, this comprehensive guide will provide valuable insights and actionable steps to help you get better at American football. This guide covers various aspects such as fitness, skill development, game strategy, and mental preparation. Step 1: Improve Your Fitness Level Explanation: Football is a physically demanding sport that places significant demands on your cardiovascular system, strength, speed, agility, and endurance. To excel in football, you must commit to improving your overall fitness level. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Engage in regular aerobic exercises (e.g., running, swimming, cycling) to build stamina and endurance. Aim for at least 30 minutes of moderate-intensity exercise per day, five days a week. * Perform resistance training twice a week to develop muscular strength and power. Focus on compound movements like squats, deadlifts, bench presses, and overhead presses. * Work on developing explosive power through plyometric exercises like jump squats, box jumps, and broad jumps. * Stretch regularly to enhance flexibility and reduce the risk of injury. Consider incorporating yoga or Pilates into your routine. Step 2: Master Fundamental Skills Explanation: To become a proficient football player, mastery of basic skills is essential. These include passing, catching, kicking, blocking, and tackling. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Passing: Practice throwing with both arms, focusing on proper grip, footwork, and follow-through. Use targets to improve accuracy. * Catching: Develop soft hands by catching passes using fingertips rather than palms. Concentrate on tracking the ball, keeping eyes on it until securely caught. * Kicking: Learn different types of kicks (placekick, punt, drop kick), focusing on technique and consistency. Seek guidance from a coach or experienced players. * Blocking: Understand body positioning, hand placement, leg drive, and sustain blocks effectively. Practice against dummy opponents or during drills. * Tackling: Emphasize form tackle techniques, ensuring head up, shoulders down, wrapping up legs, driving through the opponent, and maintaining balance throughout the process. Never lead with your helmet! Step 3: Study Game Strategy Explanation: Understanding offensive and defensive schemes is crucial for success in American football. Knowledge of playbook concepts allows players to anticipate actions, communicate more efficiently, and make quicker decisions during games. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Offensive Strategies: Familiarize yourself with common formations, routes, and protection calls. Grasp route concepts, progression reads, and pass protections. * Defensive Schemes: Learn about base defenses, coverage principles, and blitz packages. Recognizing opposing offenses can aid in making adjustments before snap counts. * Film Analysis: Watch footage of professional and college teams to identify trends, tendencies, and successful plays. Analyze individual performances to learn from top athletes. Title: How to Ask Someone Out for the First Time Introduction: Asking someone out for the first time can be a nerve-wracking experience. It's natural to feel anxious about rejection or embarrassment. However, with careful planning and consideration, you can increase your chances of success. This article will provide a comprehensive guide on how to ask someone out for the first time, including key tips and guidelines. Step 1: Choose the Right Person Before asking someone out, ensure that they are single and available. Respect their relationship status even if it means letting go of your feelings. Also, consider whether this person shares common interests or values with you. Shared hobbies or beliefs could serve as conversation starters and create bonding opportunities. Step 2: Get to Know Them Better Spend some time getting to know the person before making your move. Engage them in conversations, listen attentively to what they say, and show genuine interest in their thoughts and experiences. Building rapport is essential; people tend to accept dates from those they like and trust. Step 3: Find an Appropriate Venue Plan where you want to ask them out. A casual setting like a coffee shop or park might help reduce pressure compared to more formal settings such as restaurants. Make sure the location aligns with both of your tastes and comfort levels. Step 4: Prepare Your Approach Rehearse what you're going to say ahead of time. Practicing will make you sound confident during the actual encounter. Be clear about your intentions but avoid coming off too strong. Instead of saying "Will you go on a date with me?", try something softer yet direct like, "I really enjoy spending time with you and was wondering if you'd like to grab dinner sometime?" Step 5: Timing Matters Dere it is d' y' wan' me close it? That's okay it's no big deal at all. And that's the end of the story. Three young rats with black felt hats. Positive Integral Divisors 1 In order to understand positive integral divisors, we need to understand divisors, prime numbers and prime factorizations, and how each of these parts of number theory work with each other. Divisors, Primes, and Prime Factorization If a whole number is divided by a whole number and the quotient is a whole number, then the two numbers are called divisors of the originals number. For example, since 24 6 = 4 and all the numbers are whole numbers, we can say that 6 is a divisor of 24. Also notice that 4 is a divisor of 24 too, since we can change the order to get 24 4 = 6. Therefore, both 4 and 6 are divisors of 24. Other divisors of 24 include 1, 2, 3, 8, 12, and 24. A prime number is a positive number whose only divisors are 1 and itself. The prime factorization of a number is written as the product of the prime numbers that make up that number. Find the prime factorization of 24. The prime factorization of 24 is 2 - 2 - 2 - 3, since these are the primes that need to be multiplied to make up 24. This number can also be written in exponent form as 2 3 - 3, since it is the product of three 2's and one 3, Find the prime factorization of 64. The prime factorization of 64 is 2 - 2 - 2 - 2 - 2 - 2 = 2 6 . Find the Number of Positive Integral Divisors To find how many positive integral divisors a number has, there are two methods to choose from. In the first method, you just count the divisors mentally. We know that each number has positive integral divisors in pairs, except for perfect square numbers. If you know your number is not a perfect square, then you can count in pairs. How many positive integral divisors does 24 have? You can quickly run through the numbers, starting with 1, to find the positive integral divisors of 24. Considering pairs, we start with 1 and 24. Both of those numbers are positive integral divisors and our count is up to 2. Next is 2 and 12. The count is up to 4. After that, 3 and 8 are positive integral divisors and the count is now 6. Finally, we have 4 and 6. The count is 8. We consider 5, but 24 5 is not an integer (whole number), so it is not a positive integral divisor. The next number is 6, but we have already accounted for 6 with the 4. When you reach a number that you have already accounted for, you stop counting. Therefore, there are 8 positive integral divisors for 24. 24 Advanced Idea: Is there a way to know when to stop counting and that we have reached the last number before we start repeating numbers? Yes, there is! We know that for every positive number has a square root. The square root of a number is the number you can multiply by itself to get the original number. For example, the square root of 49 is 7 because 7 - 7 = 49. What about numbers that are not perfect squares? In this case we just need to know approximately where the square root is. The square root of 24 is approximately 4.9. Therefore, any number we try to count AFTER 4.9 is going to be a repeat from our list. Remember that 4 was the last number we tried and it was a positive integral divisor of 24, 5 was not and 6 was a repeat (from the 4). Knowing that 4.9 was the square root of 24 and thus, the limit of our search, we could have stopped at 4 and not bothered with 5 or 6. This trick can save some time. This previous method works fairly well with small numbers. The next method works will all numbers, but requires that you know or can quickly compute a number's prime factorization in exponent notation. To find the number of positive integral divisors of a number, use the following steps: Find the number of positive integral divisors of 24. Alright uh it's a gem it's a stone. Here's how to use them: Cut an index card diagonally in half to make two triangles. Take one triangle. In each corner, write a different number-for example, 12, 5, and 7. Have your child look at the card and say all the math facts that it represents with those three numbers. In this example, the math facts would be: - 5 + 7 = 12 - 7 + 5 = 12 - 12 - 7 = 5 - 12 - 5 = 7 These cards also work when your child is learning multiplication and division. Title: How to Advertise a Garage Sale on Facebook Using an Android Device Introduction: Facebook is a powerful tool that can help you reach a large audience with minimal effort. If you're planning a garage sale, advertising it on Facebook can significantly increase your chances of attracting potential buyers. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating a garage sale event and promoting it on Facebook using an Android device. Step 1: Open the Facebook App Start by unlocking your Android device and locating the Facebook app icon. Tap on the icon to open the application. Make sure you are logged into your account before proceeding. Step 2: Navigate to Your Facebook Feed Once the app is open, you should be taken directly to your news feed. Here, you will see updates from friends, pages you have liked, and suggested content based on your interests. Step 3: Access the Menu Tap on the three horizontal lines (hamburger menu) located at the top right corner of the screen. This will reveal a list of options including Marketplace, Groups, Friends, Pages, etc. Step 4: Create an Event Scroll down until you find the "Create" option and tap on "Event." Creating an event allows people to express their interest or intent to attend, making it easier for them to keep track of your sale date and time. Step 5: Enter Basic Information Fill out all necessary fields such as: - Event Name: Use something catchy like "Mega Backyard Blowout Sale!" - Location: Input your address so attendees know where to go. - Start Date & Time: Set when the sale begins and ends. - Category: Choose 'Sales & Events'. - Privacy: Select 'Public' if you want anyone to view and join; otherwise stick with 'Friends.' Step 6: Add Details About the Sale In the description box, provide more information about what kind of items will be available, any special deals or discounts, and whether there will be food or drinks offered. Be specific but concise. Step 7: Invite Guests Under the 'Invite' section, select individuals, friend lists, or communities who might be interested in attending your garage sale. Personal invitations often yield better results than general posts. Step 8: Share the Event After hitting "Save," share your newly created event on your timeline, within relevant groups, via messenger, or copy the link to post elsewhere online. The wider you cast your net, the higher the turnout is likely to be. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Timing Matters: Post your event at least one week prior to the actual date. It gives ample notice for those who plan ahead and increases visibility over time. - Engaging Visuals: Upload appealing photos showcasing some of the unique or valuable items up for grabs. Visual content tends to perform better than text alone. - Regular Updates: Keep engagement high leading up to the event by sharing sneak peeks, answering questions, and reminding followers about the upcoming sale. - Encourage Sharing: Ask your network to invite others or share the event themselves. Word-of-mouth marketing remains incredibly effective even in today's digital age. - Monitor Interactions: Pay attention to comments, reactions, shares, and joins. Respond promptly to queries and thank participants for showing interest. Pakistan is a member of the Commonwealth. However, after the war in East Pakistan the country was excluded (between 1972-1989). It was also a member between 1999 and 2007, it was excluded in 2007 for a time but again became a member in 2008. [circles over other magnets with a donut magnet] I told you it's not a garage! Decimal Word Problems Practice Questions To review these concepts, go to Decimal Word Problems Study Guide. Decimal Word Problems Practice Questions - Round 18.649 to the nearest hundredth. - Subtract: 648.99 - 21.48. - Add: 45.6 + 32.05. - Multiply: 2.8 6.51. - Divide: 230.3 23.5. - Andy had a balance of $65.58 in his bank account. He then paid a bill for $42.14. How much money did he have left in his account after he paid the bill? - Elise bought 12 cans of dog food. The price for the cans was 3 for $4.69. How much money did she spend on dog food? - At a movie theater, a box of popcorn cost $4.50, and a soda cost $2.00. If Sandra bought exactly 5 items for her family and spent $15.00, what did she buy? - Tim swam four laps of the pool in 140.8 seconds. If he swam at the same pace the entire time, how long did it take him to swim one lap? - Ken bought 12.5 gallons of gasoline at $3.12 a gallon. What is the total amount of money he spent on gasoline? - Read and understand the question. This problem asks for the total amount in a bank account after a bill has been paid. The amount in the account to start and the amount of the bill are given. - Read and understand the question. This question asks for the total amount of money spent on 12 cans of dog food. The price for 3 cans is given. - Read and understand the question. This question is looking for the exact items that were bought when the price of each is given, along with the fact that there are exactly 5 items purchased for a total of $15.00. - Read and understand the question. This question is looking for the length of time it took to swim one lap when the time to swim 4 laps is given. - Read and understand the question. This question is looking for the total amount spent on gasoline. The number of gallons and the price per gallon are given. Make a plan. The key phrase how much does he have left tells you to subtract the amount of the bill from the amount in the account. Carry out the plan. Subtract $65.58 - $42.14. Be sure to line up the decimal points. The answer is $23.44. Check your answer. Check your answer by working backward. Begin with the new balance of the account and add the amount of the bill to see if it is equal to the original amount in the bank account. $23.44 + $42.14 = $65.58. This answer is checking. This is a total of 4 $4.50 = $18, which is too high even before the 1 soda is added. Try 2 boxes of popcorn and 3 sodas. This is a total of 2 $4.50 = $9.00 and 3 $2.00 = $6.00: $9.00 + 6.00 = $15.00. This is the correct solution. Check your answer. Check your answer by making sure that the answer fits the details in the problem. Title: How to Change a Reciprocating Saw Blade **Things You'll Need:** * A reciprocating saw * New blade(s) for your reciprocating saw * Protective gloves (optional) **Step 1: Unplug or Turn Off the Saw** Before you start changing the blade, ensure that the saw is completely turned off and unplugged from the power source. This is an essential safety measure to prevent accidental activation during the process. If you have a cordless model, simply remove the battery pack. **Step 2: Locate the Blade Lock Mechanism** Reciprocating saws typically have a lock mechanism designed to hold the blade in place while you change it out. It can usually be found near the front of the tool where the blade meets the body. The exact location may vary depending on the make and model of your saw. Consult your user manual if necessary. **Step 3: Engage the Blade Lock** To engage the blade lock, press down or slide the lever into its locked position. You should feel resistance indicating that it has engaged properly. Once engaged, try wiggling the blade back and forth gently; it shouldn't move unless forced. **Step 4: Remove the Spent Blade** With the blade securely locked, locate the hexagonal nut at the end of the shaft holding the blade in place. Using a wrench provided with your saw (or another suitable tool), turn this counterclockwise until loose enough to slide the old blade out. Be careful as the exposed section of the used blade could still be sharp. Dispose of the spent blade safely. **Step 5: Prepare the New Blade** Take your new blade and align its teeth with those of the removed one so they point in the same direction. Most blades are universal but confirm compatibility with your particular model beforehand. **Step 6: Install the New Blade** Slide the new blade onto the shaft until it clicks into place. Then, using your hand or the previously used wrench, tighten the hexagonal nut clockwise until snug against the base of the blade. Avoid over-tightening as this can lead to damage or difficulty removing future blades. **Step 7: Release the Blade Lock** Disengage the blade lock by moving the lever back to its original position. Test the tension by trying to move the blade sideways - there should be minimal play. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Always wear protective gloves when handling blades to protect against cuts. * Make sure the replacement blade is compatible with your specific model of reciprocating saw. * Never forcefully insert or remove blades; if they don't fit easily, check alignment and rotation. * Keep fingers clear of the path of the blade during installation and removal. * Regularly inspect your blades for signs of wear and replace them promptly to maintain cutting efficiency and reduce risk of injury. By following these steps carefully, you'll successfully change your reciprocating saw blade safely and efficiently, ensuring optimal performance for all your DIY projects! You're going to get to ride in one of them one day. She wanted to go out to eat. Title: How to Choose What Color to Get Your Braces Introduction: Getting braces is an exciting time! They are the first steps towards achieving that perfect smile you've always wanted. One fun aspect of getting braces is choosing the color of the ligature ties - the tiny rubber bands that hold the wire onto your brackets. With so many colors available, selecting the right one can be overwhelming. This guide will help you make an informed decision about what color to choose for your braces while considering factors such as aesthetics, personality expression, and dental hygiene. Step 1: Understand the Basics Ligature ties come in various colors, which allow you to personalize your braces according to your preference. These ties need replacement during every adjustment appointment (usually every four weeks). The frequency of tie changes makes them an excellent opportunity to switch up the look of your braces if you get bored with the same color or wish to match special occasions. Key Tip: Keep in mind that clear or silver-colored ties may stain more easily from certain foods and drinks like coffee, red wine, or curry; therefore, regular cleaning and maintenance are essential to maintain their appearance. Step 2: Consider Aesthetic Preferences Consider whether you want your braces to blend in with your teeth or stand out. Clear or silver-toned ties tend to be less noticeable but might still pick up stains over time. For those who prefer making a statement, bright and bold colors like blue, green, orange, pink, purple, or red create high visibility. You could even coordinate the brace color with your clothing or school colors. Step 3: Express Personality through Colors Braces offer an unexpected way to showcase your unique personality and interests. Here are some ideas based on common hobbies and preferences: * Sports fans can opt for team colors. * Nature lovers can select earthy tones such as greens, blues, and browns. * Fashion enthusiasts can stay trendy by following popular color palettes. * Holidays provide opportunities for festive colors - think pastels for Easter, reds and greens for Christmas, etc. Step 4: Dental Hygiene Matters While having fun picking colors, don't forget about oral health. Darker shades like black, navy blue, or forest green might disguise food particles stuck between your brackets better than lighter ones. However, this doesn't mean you should neglect proper brushing and flossing habits. Always clean thoroughly around all parts of your braces after meals to prevent plaque buildup and tooth decay. Step 5: Consultation with Orthodontist Discuss your color choices with your orthodontist during appointments when they change your ties. They can provide guidance regarding any potential issues related to specific colors (e.g., dark colors causing shadows on your teeth) and ensure that your chosen shade won't interfere with treatment progress. Conclusion: Selecting the ideal color(s) for your braces allows self-expression and adds excitement to the process of straightening your teeth. By understanding the basics, considering aesthetic preferences, expressing personality, maintaining good dental hygiene, and consulting with your orthodontist, you'll enjoy showing off your vibrant new smile throughout your treatment journey. But the rose had thorns and it pricked her finger. Lily cried and ran to her mommy. "Mommy, it hurts!" she said, shivering with pain. "It's okay, Lily. You have to be careful when you pick flowers," her mommy said. Lily learned her lesson and became more independent, picking flowers on her own. But one day, she saw a big dog in the garden. The dog growled and chased her. Lily ran as fast as she could but the dog caught her and bit her leg. She cried out in pain and her mommy rushed her to the hospital. Creating a Great Artbook on MS Paint MS Paint is a simple yet powerful graphics editing software that comes pre-installed with Windows operating systems. Despite its simplicity, you can create stunning artbooks using this tool. Here's an in-depth guide on creating a great artbook on MS Paint: Step 1: Plan Your Artbook ------------------------- Before starting your artbook, plan out what you want to include. Decide on a theme or concept that ties all your artwork together. This will help give your artbook a cohesive feel and make it more appealing to viewers. Sketch rough drafts of the pages and decide on their layout. Planning ahead also allows you to determine the size of your canvas beforehand, ensuring that all your pieces fit nicely within the book format. Key Tip: Keep a consistent style throughout the artbook. While experimentation is encouraged, drastic changes in styles might confuse viewers. Step 2: Set Up Your Canvas -------------------------- Open Microsoft Paint (Start > All Programs > Accessories > Paint). To set up your canvas, click on 'Resize' under the Home tab. Change both horizontal and vertical measurements according to your planned page size. Make sure 'Pixels' is selected as the unit. Remember to leave some space around the edges for binding if you intend to physically print your artbook. Step 3: Create Guides for Page Layout ------------------------------------ To maintain consistency across spreads, create guides for margins, text areas, etc., which will act as visual cues when designing each page. Unfortunately, MS Paint doesn't support gridlines or guides like professional design software. However, you can manually draw lines using the 'Line' tool under Shapes. Step 4: Start Designing Each Page ------------------------------- Now begins the fun part - filling your pages with beautiful art! Use the various drawing tools available such as brushes, pencils, shapes, and colors. Remember to save often to avoid losing progress due to unexpected crashes or errors. Key Tips: - Experiment with different brush sizes, opacity levels, and blending options to achieve diverse effects. - Utilize layers to keep individual elements separate and easy to edit. Layer management isn't as robust in MS Paint compared to other programs, but it still offers basic functionality. - Don't forget about typography! Add captions, titles, or even poetry to enhance your visual narrative. Step 5: Review and Refine ------------------------ Once you've filled every page, take time to review your work. Look for any inconsistencies, mistakes, or areas needing improvement. Revise where necessary until satisfied with the result. Step 6: Export High-Quality Images ---------------------------------- When ready to compile your artbook, export each page as high-quality image files. Go to File > Save As... and choose either JPEG or PNG formats depending on whether you need transparency or not. Adjust the quality slider to maximum setting to preserve details during compression. Step 7: Compile into PDF Format ------------------------------ For ease of viewing and distribution, combine all pages into one PDF file. There are numerous free online services that allow you to do this quickly and efficiently. Just upload your images, adjust settings if needed, then download the final PDF version of your artbook. And there you have it - a meticulously crafted artbook made entirely using Microsoft Paint! With careful planning, creativity, and patience, nothing should limit your artistic expression. Broadly speaking, proteins have two kinds of function. The first is structural: they make up many of the key structures in cells and tissues. Muscle, hair, and skin are all mainly made of protein. The second is functional: as enzymes they greatly speed up the chemical reactions in a living cell. All cellular life consists of a thousand, or more, chemical reactions, called metabolism, which transform the molecules eaten into energy, or into other molecules the cell needs to survive. The function of proteins is to speed up these reactions, often by over a million times faster. In addition they cause chemical reactions to occur which would not without the action of the protein. [almost like "here", but not definite enough] [both Chi and MOT try money on the big magnet] Drawing a line of best fit (also known as a trendline or regression line) in Microsoft Excel can be an incredibly useful tool for analyzing data and making predictions about future trends. By following these steps, you'll be able to create your own lines of best fit with ease: 1. **Prepare Your Data**: Before creating a line of best fit, ensure that your data is organized in columns or rows within an Excel worksheet. Each column should represent a single variable, and there should be at least two columns for the x-axis and y-axis variables. The more data points you have, the more accurate your line of best fit will be. 2. **Select the Data Points**: Select all the data points by clicking and dragging across the cells containing the relevant information. Make sure to include both x and y values. This selection determines which data points are used when calculating the equation of the line of best fit. 3. **Insert Trendline**: Navigate to the 'Chart Tools - Design' tab, then click on the 'Add Chart Element' button. From the dropdown menu, select 'Trendline', followed by 'More Trendline Options...' to open the Format Trendline pane. Alternatively, right-click on any of the selected data points and choose 'Add Trendline...'. 4. **Choose Type of Regression**: Within the Format Trendline pane, under 'Trendline options', choose the type of regression line suitable for your dataset from the 'Type' dropdown list. For most cases, either linear or logarithmic regressions would suffice; however, if needed, other types like polynomial, power, or moving average could also be chosen based on specific requirements. 5. **Customize Appearance & Settings**: You can customize various aspects such as appearance (color, dash type), display equation on chart, set forecast period etc., depending upon what suits your needs. - *Display Equation*: Check this box to show the equation of the line of best fit directly on your chart. This can help you understand the relationship between the variables being analyzed. - *Set Forecast Period*: If you want to predict future values using the regression line, enter a number greater than zero into this field. Excel will then extend the line beyond your existing data points to estimate unknown values. 6. **Apply Changes**: Click 'Close' to close the Format Trendline pane and return to your chart. The line of best fit now appears overlaid on top of your original data points. It visually represents the general direction and pattern in which the data moves, helping identify potential correlations or trends. 7. **Interpret Results**: Interpreting results involves understanding both visual cues provided by the graph itself along with numerical measures associated with the created trendline. Key indicators like R value provide insights regarding strength and nature of correlation between variables. Remember though, correlation does not imply causation! By mastering this skill, you significantly enhance your ability to analyze patterns and make informed decisions backed up by quantitative evidence. Happy graphing! [Sarah is sitting at the kitchen table drinking orange juice] August Theme: Motivating Readers Recommended Grades: 3rd-5th grades In Big Plans a little boy sits in the corner of his classroom plotting his future and his plans are BIG! With the help of his bird and a lucky stinky hat, he may become Mayor, President, or fly to the moon! A great book to use to guide students to think about their own big plans. This book could be a great discussion starter at the beginning of the school year to elicit student plans for the year and to motivate them to create plans. This book can be used in a number of ways: Lesson Plan Ideas: Goal Setting: After reading aloud this book, discuss the ideas of making goals with your students. As a class make a list of goals to complete for the year. Post them prominently so students can see when goals are achieved. In addition, ask students to make individual goals. For younger students, this may only mean one or two goals - or conduct a shared writing with younger students to determine their goals. Older students should be able to make four or five. Don't get caught up in the number of goals though, each child will be different. Throughout the year, revisit the class goals and individual goals and determine how to meet them and if they have been met. In addition, students may want to revise their goals or add new ones. Writers Workshop: After reading aloud this book, discuss the idea of having dreams and goals (BIG plans!). Ask your students to create a list in their writers notebook of their dreams and goals (no matter how far fetched!). Later on in the year, when students need a writing piece, they can choose one of their dreams or goals to elaborate on. Perhaps they will want to write down the steps they may need to take to reach a goal. Or, if a goal has already been met, perhaps they would like to share what they did to meet their goal. 2010 by Dawn Little for Picture This! Teaching with Picture Books. You sit down here on Mommy's stool. Either while he's standing or sitting down. The atmosphere is in constant motion on scales ranging from local gusts to wind belts that encircle the globe. Hot air rises. As air rises, air pressure at the surface is lowered. Rising air expands and cools (adiabatic cooling: that is, it cools due to change in volume as opposed to adding or taking away of heat). The result is condensation/precipitation. Cold air sinks. Sinking air compresses and heats. As air sinks, air pressure at the surface is raised. Cold air holds less moisture than warm. Air travels from high to low pressure, creating wind. If the Earth did not rotate, what would wind patterns look like? Just like convection currents with air rising at the equator and sinking at the poles. However, the real wind patterns are broken into several convection cells due to the Coriolis effects. So on a rotating Earth, wind is controlled by: [Disney Phone says "Hi this is Mickey Mouse"] [name puzzle pieces as Chi puts them in] he cried. "It can be seen--Madame has a temperament." Truce: The Day the Soldiers Stopped Fighting Booktalk - Grades: 6-8 About this book None of them wanted to fight on Christmas. So, they didn't. In December, 1914, the world was at war, and the soldiers on both sides were tired of fighting. They were huddled in trenches from 50 to 1000 yards apart, that stretched in parallel lines across France, from the North Sea south, all the way to Switzerland. The land between the trenches was called No Man's Land, and in many places, at night after the shelling stopped, the men from both sides could hear their enemies' conversations, laughter, the coughing or groaning of the sick and wounded, and occasionally voices singing. And as they listened, the men on both sides began to see the other side not as the monsters their commanders had described, but men just like themselves, familiar, even friendly. They were all tired of this war, and missed their families and their homelands. They'd been fighting for six months, and more than anything else, they just wanted the war to stop. They'd been told that they'd be home for Christmas, but it was obvious they wouldn't. And they all wondered what would happen on Christmas Day. Gift boxes began to arrive as families accepted the inevitable. Spirits lifted, and the fighting slowed down, in spite of orders from the commanders of the two armies, German and Allied (British, French), that it should continue. On Christmas Eve, the gunshots and artillery gradually stopped. The temperature had dropped suddenly the night before, and the mud in the trenches and the ground of No Man's Land were frozen solid, and covered with a thin layer of Christmas snow. Carols sung by first one side and then the other, floated across No Man's Land. Cautiously, men began to emerge from their trenches, and when no shots were fired, walked out toward the enemy lines. Men who had tried to kill each other, instead shook hands, shared gifts from home, and forgot about the war. It was late at night when the men turned back to their own lines, calling goodbye and promising not to fight the next day. Christmas Day dawned to a thick fog and an eerie silence. Then a voice rang out from the German side, "You no shoot, we no shoot." It was a Christmas no one in either army would ever forget. A Christmas when men made friends with their enemy, instead of shooting them. A Christmas when the soldiers on the front lines defied their commanders, and said, "I will not fight. I will see no one as my enemy." Could it be that the end of war is just this simple? "No, I will not fight." This booktalk was written by Dr. Joni R. Title: How to Climb Blocks on Roblox Introduction: In this extensive tutorial, we will guide you through the process of climbing blocks in Roblox. This skill can be helpful in various scenarios within the game, such as reaching high platforms or traversing challenging terrains. We'll provide detailed steps along with useful tips and guidelines to ensure your success. Step 1: Understand the Basics of Movement in Roblox Before attempting to climb blocks, familiarize yourself with basic movement controls. By default, the 'W', 'A', 'S', and 'D' keys are used for moving forward, left, backward, and right respectively. The spacebar allows players to jump. These movements form the foundation for climbing blocks effectively. Step 2: Approach the Block Identify the block you want to climb and position yourself directly in front of it. Make sure there is enough room above the block for your character to ascend. Key Tip: Try starting with smaller blocks until you get comfortable with the technique. Larger blocks may require more advanced methods which we'll discuss later in this tutorial. Step 3: Jump Onto the Block Use the spacebar to make your character leap towards the block. Your goal here is to land on top of the block instead of just next to it. Timing is crucial; if you press the spacebar too early or late, you might miss the block entirely. Guideline: As soon as you reach the edge of the block, hit the spacebar. This timing ensures that your character jumps vertically onto the block rather than horizontally past it. Step 4: Adjust Position (Optional) If necessary, adjust your horizontal position by using the 'A' and 'D' keys. Align yourself centrally over the block so that when you attempt to move upwards, you won't fall off due to being positioned too close to an edge. Step 5: Prepare for Next Step Now that you're standing stably on the first block, look at the adjacent higher block you wish to reach. You need to judge whether you can simply jump straight up to grab it or if you have to perform additional actions like jumping from another angle or stacking multiple blocks. Step 6: Execute the Leap Press the spacebar again to propel your character upward. While mid-air, use either the 'W', 'A', or 'D' keys depending on where the next block is located relative to yours. If executed correctly, you should latch onto the second block upon landing. Key Tips: * Practice good judgment about the distance between the two blocks before leaping. * Remember that pressing the directional keys ('W', 'A', 'D') while airborne changes the trajectory of your jump. Step 7: Repeat Process Keep repeating these steps until you reach your desired height. Be mindful of any potential dangers overhead, especially if other players could knock you down during multiplayer games. Advanced Techniques: For larger gaps or taller structures, consider building makeshift staircases out of available blocks. Carefully place each block end-to-end leading up to your target location. Then slowly ascend following our previously described techniques. Once done, don't forget to destroy those temporary stairs behind you to avoid confusion for others and maintain cleanliness within the game environment. Conclusion: Congratulations! With patience and practice, mastering block climbing in Roblox opens up new possibilities for exploration and strategic play. Keep refining your skills and stay tuned for future guides offering even more advanced strategies. Then she told him she would be with him and aid him in the coming strife. He did not see Siegmund at first, for a heavy storm had come up, while the heavens seemed rent with terrific crashes of thunder. Title: How to Change Your Phone Number on Telegram for Android Telegram is a popular messaging app that offers end-to-end encrypted communication. If you've changed your phone number and want to continue using Telegram with your new number, this tutorial will guide you through changing your number while keeping all your messages, contacts, and groups intact. This process can be done directly from your Android device following these steps: 1. Open the Telegram App: Locate the Telegram app icon (a blue paper airplane) on your home screen or app drawer and tap on it. It will open to the 'Chats' tab if you are already logged into your account. 2. Access Settings Menu: Tap on the three horizontal lines at the top left corner of the screen to reveal the side menu. Scroll down and select 'Settings'. You will now see several options related to customizing your Telegram experience. 3. Go to Edit Profile: From the settings page, find and tap 'Edit profile', which should be located near the top of the list under your name and current phone number. Editing your profile allows you to modify various aspects like name, picture, about section, and contact information. 4. Initiate Changing Phone Number: On the edit profile page, look for the field displaying your existing phone number. There should be an option button next to it; usually represented by a pencil icon. Pressing this button enables editing your phone number. 5. Enter New Phone Number: Delete your old phone number by selecting every digit until the text box becomes empty. Now type in your new phone number including country code but excluding any exit codes (e.g., +1 for North America). Make sure there are no spaces or special characters between digits. After entering the full number, click 'Save' or the checkmark icon typically found at the upper right corner of the keyboard. 6. Verify Identity Through SMS Code: As part of its security measures, Telegram requires verification of your identity via an SMS code sent to your newly inputted phone number. Retrieve the 5-digit code from your message inbox and enter it in the provided space within the app. Once entered correctly, press 'Next' or the arrow icon pointing forward. 7. Associate Contacts With New Number: Upon successful verification, you will have two choices - 'Merge Accounts' or 'Add Contact'. Merge accounts combines your chat history, contacts, and groups with your previous account so they remain accessible. Selecting 'Add Contact' only adds your new number as another method for people to reach you without merging past data. Choose accordingly based on whether you wish to retain historical conversations and connections. 8. Notify Contacts About Changes: Depending on your choice in Step 7, you may receive prompts regarding informing selected or all contacts about your updated phone number. Decide whether you would like to notify them immediately or manually later. Remember that updating your number might affect active Secret Chats where self-destruct timers have been set since both parties must confirm their identities after such changes. 9. Complete Process: Congratulations! By completing these steps, you have successfully changed your phone number associated with your Telegram account on your Android device. Continue enjoying secure chatting with friends, family, and colleagues across various devices. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always double-check the accuracy of your new phone number before saving and verifying to avoid future complications. * Be mindful when choosing whether to merge accounts or add a new one, considering potential impacts on conversation histories and group memberships. * Keep track of active Secret Chats and anticipate possible effects resulting from your change in phone number. Hiring a communications lawyer is an important decision that can significantly impact your business or personal interests. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, providing in-depth explanations and key tips along the way. Step 1: Determine Your Need for a Communications Lawyer Communications law covers various aspects related to telecommunications, internet, broadcasting, and media. If you're involved in any activity within these areas, hiring a communications lawyer might be necessary to ensure compliance with regulations and protect your rights. Key indicators include: * You operate a telecom company or provide communication services. * You own intellectual property used in mass communication (e.g., music, movies, TV shows). * You're planning to launch a new product or service that involves data privacy issues. * You need advice on mergers, acquisitions, or partnerships involving communication companies. * You face litigation regarding defamation, invasion of privacy, or freedom of speech. Step 2: Identify Potential Candidates Start by asking for referrals from colleagues, industry peers, or professional organizations. Online directories such as Martindale-Hubbell and Justia can also help identify potential candidates. Look for attorneys who specialize in communications law and have experience dealing with cases similar to yours. Key Tips: * Check their credentials - Make sure they are licensed to practice in your state and federal courts if applicable. * Review their track record - Have they won cases like yours before? Are there negative reviews online? Step 3: Schedule Initial Consultations Most lawyers offer free initial consultations. Use this opportunity to discuss your situation, ask questions, and assess whether you feel comfortable working with them. Prepare a list of topics to cover during the meeting, including: * Their understanding of your issue(s) * Relevant laws and regulations * Possible strategies and outcomes * Estimated costs and timeline Key Guidelines: * Be honest about all facts surrounding your case * Take notes during the consultation * Ask about next steps after the consultation Step 4: Evaluate Compatibility After meeting with several lawyers, evaluate which one best fits your needs based on factors like expertise, rapport, communication skills, and cost structure. Remember, you'll likely be sharing sensitive information with this person, so feeling at ease with them is crucial. Step 5: Understand Fees & Billing Before making a final decision, clarify how billing works. Some common fee arrangements include hourly rates, flat fees, contingency fees, and retainers. Each has its pros and cons, depending on the complexity and expected duration of your case. Don't hesitate to negotiate terms that suit both parties. Step 6: Sign the Engagement Letter Once you've chosen a lawyer, they should send you an engagement letter outlining the scope of representation, fees, confidentiality agreement, and other relevant details. Read it carefully, ask questions if anything isn't clear, then sign and return it. Congratulations! You've hired a communications lawyer. Key Tips: * Keep copies of everything signed and received * Regularly communicate updates and concerns with your attorney * Trust their guidance but stay informed throughout the process 'Cause the other one the stuff is comin' out of it Adam. Find the Oghma Infinium in Skyrim: A Comprehensive Guide The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim is an action role-playing video game developed by Bethesda Game Studios. One of the many intriguing quests in this vast open world is finding the legendary book known as the Oghma Infinium. This powerful artifact holds knowledge that can significantly boost your character's skills. Here's our in-depth guide on how to find the Oghma Infinium in Skyrim. Step 1: Begin the Discerning the Transmundane Quest To start the journey towards obtaining the Oghma Infinium, you must first trigger the "Discerning the Transmundale" quest. To do so, approach the mysterious orb called the Lexicon located at the College of Winterhold. Once activated, the orb will teleport you to a strange location where you'll meet Septimus Signus. Interact with him to begin the quest. Key Tips: - Make sure you have high speech or illusion skills to persuade Septimus if needed. - Save before talking to Septimus; he might become hostile depending upon your choices during dialogue. Step 2: Retrieve the Dwemer Cube from Mzulft Septimus requests that you bring back a Dwemer cube to help unlock the true potential of the lexicon. Travel to Mzulft, northwest of Windhelm, and delve into its extensive ruins. You are looking for an object called 'Dwarven Mechanism'. After acquiring it, return to Septimus. Guidelines: - Use Detect Life spell to locate hidden enemies within the ruins. - Take out any threats silently using stealth attacks or bow & arrow. - Keep an eye out for traps while navigating through narrow corridors and rooms. Step 3: Assist Septimus Further After returning the Dwemer cube, engage in further conversation with Septimus. He will then ask you to retrieve the Focus Points which include Bloodscry Crystal, Lunar Forge Key, and Stellar Core. These items are scattered across various locations - Blackreach, Tower of Mzark (Skyrim), and Labyrinthian (Bleak Falls Barrow). Important Notes: - Each locale presents unique challenges like dangerous creatures, puzzles, and treacherous paths. Be prepared accordingly. - It may be wise to level up appropriately before venturing these areas. Step 4: Return to Septimus with all Three Focus Points Once you gather all three focus points, head back to Septimus. Upon presenting them, he combines these components along with the lexicon resulting in the creation of the Oghma Infinium. However, there's one final challenge awaiting... Step 5: Choose wisely among Paths of Knowledge Now comes the crucial part - deciding how to utilize the power contained within the Oghma Infinium. There are three paths available: The Path of Might, The Path of Shadow, and The Path of Magic. Selecting any path grants a permanent +5 bonus to seven related skills but reduces another unrelated skill by 5 points permanently. Carefully consider what benefits align best with your playstyle. Final Thoughts: Finding the Oghma Infinium requires exploration, combat, puzzle-solving, and decision making. Remember always to save often, especially when interacting with significant characters like Septimus Signus. Good luck on your epic adventure! yeah, why isn't Fakir in the first bunch? We're gettin' bomb threats at work weekly. Figure It Out With "Ehh?" Rationale: One of the most important factors in predicting reading success in students is their ability to recognize phonemes. This lesson will teach the letter e and the phoneme /e/ with a gesture, tongue twister, and letterbox lesson. Picture with an old man trying to hear to represent /e/ Strip with tongue twister written out: "Emmy the Eskimo ate eggs on the elevator." Letterbox strip for each student List of words for students to spell: Ed, egg, let, bed, mesh, lend. Letter tiles for each student: e, d, g, g, l, t, b, m, s, h Red Gets Fed List of paired words for evaluation 1. Introduce the lesson. "Class, today we are going to learn a new sound." Write an e on the board. "This is the letter e. It makes the /e/ sound, like in egg. Can every make that sound with me? Good!" 2. Introduce the gesture that goes with "e." "Let's think about the sound we make when we are trying to hear someone better. We say ehhh? Try this sound with me. Great job! Every time we make this sound, lets put our hand to our ear like we're trying to hear someone. Repeat after me. Ehhh? Now you go. Good!" 3. Go over tongue twister. Model the sentence by reading it first and then having the students read it normally. The next time they read it, we will add in the hand gesture and the students will exaggerate the /e/ sound. "Now I am going to show you all a silly tongue twister for us to practice our new sound." Put up strip with "Emmy the Eskimo ate eggs on the elevator." Read strip for students and have them repeat. "Now watch me read the sentence and try to listen with our hand every time I hear the /e/ sound. Eeeemy the Eeeskimo ate eeegs on the eeelevator. Now you all try. Don't forget to use our hand motion!" 4. Have students ready for letterbox when we start the lesson. Have model box and letters written on the board. Model the word "blend" on the board. Have students work their list of words, observing and assisting when needed. "Okay boys and girls, we are going to do a letter box lesson similar to the one we did for the /a/ last week. First, watch me spell the word blend. Bbbblllleeeeennndddd. First, I hear the /b/ sound so I will place a "b" in the first box. Next I hear /l/, then eeeee oh, /e/, our new sound today! After that, I hear /n/ and /d/ [place letters in box as I am talking]. Now you all are going to try a few words." Before calling out each word, have students change the number of boxes on their letterboxes. After I call out each word, give students time to solve it in their boxes. Check each word before moving on. List of words: 2- [Ed]. 3- [egg, let, bed]. 4- [mesh, lend]. Letters for each student: e, d, g, g, l, t, b, m, s, h. 5. After we finish the LBL, we will read Red Gets Fed. "Okay boys and girls, now that we can spell and read some short e words, we are now going to read a book with our new /e/ sound. This book is about a little puppy who really wants his dinner! He asks everyone in the family- will anyone give him his food? Let's read together to find out. Every time you come across the /e/ sound, use your hand motion with your ear to figure out the sound." 6. Once we finish the book, I will have each student write a message on primary paper. "Now that we have read our book, we are going to write a message! Because we just read about a dog eating dinner, we are going to write about a special dinner you have had with your family." The students should use inventive spelling when they write, and this will help me evaluate if they can make a connection with the story, as well as their phonemic awareness. Once students finish writing, they can draw a picture of their story. I will have the students come to me individually while they working on their message. They will listen to some pairs of words and then tell me which ones have the /e/ sound. "I have some pairs of words that I am going to read to you. For example, do you hear the /e/ sound in "map" or "wet"? How about "cot" or "Ted"? "Cake" or "shed"? "Let" or "read"? Evaluate how well students know the short /e/ sound. Return to Doorways Index. Title: How to Earn 1000 Coins on Jetpack Adventure Jetpack Adventure is an addictive game where you control a character with a jetpack, trying to collect coins while avoiding obstacles. This tutorial will guide you through earning 1000 coins in this exciting game. Follow these steps carefully and enjoy your gaming experience! **Step 1:** Familiarize Yourself With The Game Controls Before diving into coin collection, take some time to learn the basic controls of Jetpack Adventure. - **Movement**: Use arrow keys or touchscreen controls to move left or right. - **Jumping**: Tap the up arrow key or jump button to activate the jetpack and ascend. Release to descend. Understanding the controls thoroughly ensures that you can focus more on collecting coins rather than figuring out how to navigate. **Step 2:** Plan Your Route Observe the layout of the level before starting your run. Look for patterns in the terrain and plan where you'll fly to maximize coin collection without crashing. Keep an eye out for high-value clusters of coins - aim for those first! **Step 3:** Master Takeoff and Landing Practice taking off smoothly by pressing the jump button just as your character touches the ground. Similarly, practice landing gently by releasing the jump button early enough so that your character doesn't slam into the surface. Smooth starts and stops help maintain speed and prevent accidents caused by abrupt changes in direction or height. **Step 4:** Maintain Speed Moving quickly allows you to cover greater distances and reach higher platforms, thus enabling more opportunities for coin gathering. However, be careful not to go too fast; maintaining control is crucial for successful play. **Step 5:** Prioritize Gold Coins Over Silver Ones Gold coins are worth twice as much as silver ones (10 vs 5). Therefore, prioritize them when making decisions about which coins to grab. If possible, try to avoid slowing down excessively to pick up lower-value silvers if there are golds nearby. **Step 6:** Utilize Boosters Strategically Some levels have boosters scattered throughout. These give temporary speed increases but consume coins per second. Only use them when necessary, such as reaching distant groups of coins or escaping dangerous situations. Remember, wasting coins reduces overall earnings. **Step 7:** Practice Risk Management Knowing when to take risks and when to play safe is vital in any game, including Jetpack Adventure. While going after hard-to-reach coins might seem lucrative, sometimes it's better to stick to safer paths. Balance risky moves with cautious play to optimize earnings. **Step 8:** Stay Focused and Patient Collecting 1000 coins isn't easy and requires patience and concentration. Don't get discouraged by initial failures. Each attempt provides valuable learning experiences. Stay focused, keep practicing, and soon enough, you'll hit that magical number! By following these eight steps meticulously, you're well on your way to amassing 1000 shiny coins in Jetpack Adventure. Happy gaming! Maoism was a theory created because of Mao's beliefs and ideologies. Similar to Karl Marx, he agrees that a proletariat revolution is essential in order for society to change. As opposed to Marx who states that the factory workers should revolutionize, Mao felt that the farmers in China were the ones in need of this revolution. Marx supports an economically strong state that is industrialized. Mao on the other hand, does not support industrialization or technology. XIII [very soft; 3 sec silence, car passes, slam; stirring] Swords were first created from bronze by blacksmiths in Ancient Egypt in 1150 BCE. Soon other cultures adopted them, and they began to spread quite quickly. Before guns were invented, swords were much more common as a weapon. After the invention of the gun, swords remained as "sidearms", as secondary weapons used in man-to-man fight once the battle lines had contacted after shooting. Since after the American Civil War, swords have not been used as much by armies, except as a ceremonial part of uniforms. Some guns, however, have bayonets to perform the same function. Title: How to Bond With Your Children: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Building a strong bond with your children is crucial for their emotional, social, and cognitive development. It provides them with a sense of security, increases their self-esteem, and fosters open communication. This tutorial will offer you actionable steps, backed by research and expert opinions, to help you create and strengthen your connection with your kids. By following these guidelines consistently over time, you can develop a deep and meaningful relationship that lasts a lifetime. Step 1: Spend Quality Time Together Explanation: Engaging in activities together allows parents and children to build memories and understand one another better. Shared experiences promote trust, attachment, and positive feelings between parent and child. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Schedule regular one-on-one time with each child based on their interests and preferences. * Participate in hobbies or sports they enjoy, even if you're not an expert yourself. * Create new traditions like family game nights, weekend outings, or annual vacations. * Limit distractions such as phones, TVs, or computers during quality time. Step 2: Show Unconditional Love and Support Explanation: Demonstrating love without conditions teaches children that they are valued and accepted regardless of their achievements or mistakes. This encourages honesty, resilience, and mental wellbeing. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Express affection through words, hugs, kisses, and physical touch. * Praise effort rather than just results, promoting a growth mindset. * Listen actively when your child speaks, showing empathy and understanding. * Avoid criticizing or punishing harshly; instead, teach problem-solving skills and natural consequences. Step 3: Encourage Open Communication Explanation: Creating a safe space for conversations enables parents to understand their children's thoughts, emotions, and concerns while teaching valuable communication skills. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Initiate discussions about various topics, including school, friends, and personal struggles. * Practice active listening techniques like maintaining eye contact, nodding, summarizing, and asking questions. * Validate your child's feelings and perspectives, even if you disagree. * Be transparent about age-appropriate information regarding your own life and experiences. Step 4: Establish Boundaries and Consistency Explanation: Clear expectations and routines provide structure, predictability, and safety, which contributes to healthy emotional development and stronger bonds. Key Tips & Guidelines: Did you get dressed up for trick-or-treat? Um I don't think it will stick. Title: How to Choose the Perfect Tamagotchi Introduction: Tamagotchis are electronic pets that have been popular among children and adults alike since their creation in 1996. With so many different models and variations available, choosing the perfect Tamagotchi can be overwhelming. This guide will walk you through the process of selecting the ideal Tamagotchi based on your preferences and needs. Step 1: Determine Your Experience Level If you're new to Tamagotchis or digital pets, start with a basic model like the Tamagotchi Pix or Tamagotchi On. These devices offer simple interfaces and caretaking tasks suitable for beginners. More experienced users might prefer advanced models such as the Tamagotchi Meets or Tamagotchi iD L, which provide additional features and complexity. Key tip: Research each device online to learn about its interface, functions, and difficulty level before making a decision. Step 2: Decide on Desired Connectivity Options Some newer Tamagotchi models allow connectivity via Bluetooth or infra-red technology. Consider if connecting with other players is important to you. If so, choose a model compatible with other devices, such as the Tamagotchi On or Tamagotchi Friends. Keep in mind that these features may increase the price. Guideline: Make sure both parties involved agree on using the same connection method (Bluetooth or IR) when planning to exchange data between two Tamagotchis. Step 3: Select a Theme That Appeals To You There are numerous themes available across various Tamagotchi models. From food-themed ones like the Mametchi Cafe to fashion-focused designs like the Tamagotchi Town, there is something for everyone. Pick a theme that aligns with your interests to ensure an enjoyable experience. Key tip: Some limited edition themed Tamagotchis hold higher resale values due to collector interest. Look into potential future value if this aspect matters to you. Step 4: Assess Battery Life Expectancy Battery life varies significantly depending on usage and type. Most modern Tamagotchis utilize CR2032 batteries, while some older versions require LR44 cells. Check user reviews to estimate battery longevity per charge cycle. Choosing a model with longer battery life reduces replacement frequency and costs over time. Guideline: Remember to turn off your Tamagotchi when not in use to conserve battery power. Step 5: Compare Price Points Prices range from budget options under $20 to high-end collectibles reaching hundreds of dollars. Set a realistic budget according to your financial means and desired features. While pricier models often boast more capabilities, affordable alternatives still deliver engaging experiences without breaking the bank. Key tip: Monitor prices at multiple retailers, including online marketplaces, specialty shops, and official distributors, to secure the best deal possible. Conclusion: Selecting the right Tamagotchi requires careful consideration of factors such as experience levels, connectivity options, themes, battery life, and pricing. By following this comprehensive guide, you can make an informed choice tailored to your specific tastes and requirements, ensuring countless hours of entertainment with your virtual pet companion. From then on, Timmy made sure to keep his room clean and modern, and he always did his tasks right away. He didn't want anything bad to happen again. The skull is a "brain bucket." It holds and protects the brain, eyeballs, nose and ears. The skull is made of the face bones and the protective helmet called the cranium. The cranium is made of 8 flat bones that are connected by sutures. The sutures are simply bumpy edges that fit together like puzzle pieces. They are called sutures because they have the appearance of stitches (another word for sutures). The face bones form the base for the soft tissues of the face. There are holes in the face bones for the eye sockets and nose (i.e., the nasal cavity). The mandible forms the lower jaw, which can drop downward to open the mouth. The sinuses are air pockets within the skull. The sinuses reduce the overall weight of the skull without a big decrease in the skull's strength. The sinuses also help the voice reverberate, making it louder and clearer. This is similar to the effect of an acoustic guitar. When a child has sinusitis and the sinuses are filled with fluid, the voice becomes muffled. Ethmoid bone - A bone that is hidden within the nasal cavity. It forms the nasal septum (the wall between the left and right nostrils) and the roof of the nose, called the cribriform plate. The cribriform plate has many holes through which the olfactory nerves pass. These nerves provide the sense of smell. Frontal bone - The forehead bone. Forms the upper part of the eye socket underneath the eyebrows. Occipital bone - Forms the back of the cranium and provides the attachment point for the spine. A large hole in the occipital bone, called the foramen magnum, provides an opening for the spinal cord. Parietal bones (right and left) - Provide a roof and side walls for the cranium. Temporal bones (right and left) - Form the lower sides of the cranium. Beneath the temples and has holes on each side for the acoustic meatus (the ear opening). Provides an attachment point for the jawbone (mandible). Sphenoid bone - A butterfly-shaped bone that helps form the floor of the cranium and part of the eye socket walls. Contains a small, saddle-shaped sunken area called the sella turcica which protects the pituitary gland. Lacrimal bones - Small plate-like bones that help form the inside walls of the eyesocket. These bones contain a tube (called the nasolacrimal duct) through which tears from the eyes are drained into the nose. Mandible - The jaw bone. This bone moves up and down as you open and close your mouth. It holds your lower teeth and is the only bone of the skull that moves. Maxilla - The bone that holds your upper teeth. Nasal bone - The upper part (or bridge) of the nose. This is the hard part of the upper nose. The lower part of the nose and nostrils contains cartilage. Palantine bones - Help form the floor of the nose, roof of the mouth and part of the nasal septum. Vomer bone - Deep within the nose, it helps form the nasal septum. Zygomatic bones - Helps form the cheek with the zygomatic arch and form the lateral sides of the eye socket. The sinuses are air pockets within the skull. The sinuses reduce the overall weight of the skull without a big decrease in the skull's strength. The sinuses also help the voice reverberate, making it louder and clearer. This is similar to the effect of an acoustic guitar. When a child has sinusitis and the sinuses are filled with fluid, the voice becomes muffled. See article on sinuses. In military usage, the word "firearm" is used for the type of weapons that soldiers carry. Most of them are either pistols or rifles. Guns that are small enough to be carried and used by one person have the same basic parts: a trigger, a barrel, and a handgrip or stock. The trigger is pulled by a finger to fire the weapon which releases a bullet. The barrel guides the bullet which then flies to the target. The handgrip is held by the shooter's hand. Guns can be very effective weapons. Their bullets fly faster than an arrow or spear. Their high kinetic energy makes them more lethal. They also shoot from far distances. Bullets are usually non-explosive pieces of metal. You said she's more interested in you than she is in the toys. Christmastide, as it is often called, is in the winter of the Northern Hemisphere, at a time when there were already ancient festivals. Some of the traditions that are used for Christmas are older than Christmas, or come from other non-Christian traditions such as Yule. Modern traditions of Christmas often focus on the giving of gifts. The season for retail stores to sell gifts, food, greeting cards, Christmas trees, and decorations begins the day about a month before Christmas Day. Horst K hler (born 22 February 1943) is a German politician of the Christian Democratic Union. He was President of Germany from 2004 to 2010. He was the candidate of the two Christian Democratic sister parties, the CDU and the CSU, and the liberal FDP, for becoming President. K hler was elected to his first five-year term by the Federal Assembly on 23 May 2004. He was reelected to a second term on 23 May 2009. About one year later, on 31 May 2010, he resigned from his office in a controversy over his comment on the role of the Bundeswehr in light of a visit to the troops in Afghanistan. He ran every day around the farm, and the other animals would cheer him on. One day, Sam was running faster and harder than ever before. He went faster than he meant to and tripped over a rock. He hurt his foot and couldn't run anymore. Sam cried and said, "My foot hurts, who will fix it for me?" Just then, an elephant came walking by. He said to Sam, "I know how to fix your foot. I can make it strong again. Would you like me to try?" Sam nodded his head yes and the elephant said, "Let me get to work!" The elephant got some herbs from the farmer and put them on Sam's foot. He rubbed them in and said, "Now your foot should feel fit in no time!" And sure enough, it did! Sam thanked the elephant and said, "Now I can run again!" The elephant smiled and said, "You're welcome!" Sam was quite happy, and ran around the farm as fast as ever. Throughout your life, you will have two sets of teeth: primary (baby) teeth and secondary (permanent) teeth. At age 6-8 months, the primary teeth appear; all 20 are in place by age 3. Permanent teeth will begin to grow around age 6, and except for wisdom teeth, are all present between ages 12 and 14. The next teeth to grow in are the 12-year molars and finally the wisdom teeth. Wisdom teeth typically begin breaking through from age 17 and on. The total number of permanent teeth is 32, though few people have room for all 32 teeth. This is why wisdom teeth are usually removed. Your front teeth are called incisors. The sharp "fang-like" teeth are canines. The next side teeth are referred to as pre-molars or bicuspids, and the back teeth are molars. Your permanent teeth are the ones you keep for life, so it is vital that they are brushed and flossed regularly and that periodic check-ups by a dentist are followed. Writing Poems by "Talking Back" One of the best ways to learn how to construct a simple poem is to take someone else's poem and use it as your structure. This is not cheating or copying, because you write your own version with your own ideas. For example your students could write their own poems based on William Carlos Williams' poem The Red Wheelbarrow, but instead write about a topic of their choice. They use Willams' simple one or two word lines as a starting point. For a whole book on this topic, try Talking Back to Poems by Daniel Alderson. It has lots of examples and poems to use. A haiku is a three-line poem with a total of 17 syllables (5/7/5). There are websites such as this that explain how to write a haiku and give a number of examples. Along with haikus, your students can then go on to write cinquains and tankas. These are all simple short poems with some rules to make it more fun! First Lines/Last Lines Give your students several first and last lines to choose from, then ask them to write a poem using the lines they have picked. If you like, you can use lines from published poems, or make up your own, or use mine: This was not the day Beneath the bridge The photos on the piano In the small town, on the widest street In the mirror You must not call me and the sound went on. you ended with nothing. painted on the wall. like the black night. too many flies in the soup. only you will know the answer. in the box. There are many ways to use repetition in a poem: 1. Choose one word (e.g. nose, which can also be knows or nos) and use it at least ten times. It's good to use a word that sounds the same but is spelled differently and has different meanings. 2. Repeat the same word or words at the beginning of each line. E.g. Someday I will, Can you hear, You will be, When I am - these are simple ones. 3. Write a line that can act as a refrain, then use it three or four times throughout the poem. Write a poem in which you compare two opposite things (such as rocks and water, or smile and frown), or a poem about two completely different things (such as car and cloud, or tennis ball and snail). It's often better to use concrete objects or actions rather than emotions or abstract concepts such as honesty or courage. I stand up and I hold on like this. Moresnet or Neutral Moresnet () was a very small territory that existed from 1816 to 1919. It had a size of 3.5 km . It existed because its neighbours could not agree on who should own it, so they decided to make it a neutral territory where both neighbours would both have power. It was 7 km Southwest of Aix-la-Chapelle (Germany), and south of the points were the borders of Germany, Belgium and the Netherlands come together on the Vaalserberg. Potential energy is often associated with restoring forces such as a spring or the force of gravity. The action of stretching the spring or lifting the mass is performed by an external force that works against the force field of the potential. This work is stored in the force field, which is said to be stored as potential energy. If the external force is removed the force field acts on the body to perform the work as it moves the body back to the initial position, reducing the stretch of the spring or causing a body to fall. When this happens, potential energy changes into kinetic energy. The total energy stays the same because of the law of conservation of energy. It is played with the standard 52 cards in four suits. The game developed out of whist, which is a relatively old card game. Bridge developed in a series of stages from about 1890 to about 1930. The stages included bridge-whist and auction bridge, and the developments took place mostly in New York and London. The origin of the word "bridge" in this context is not really known. One possibility is that it is taken from an 1886 book, "Biritch, or Russian Whist" by John Collinson. [pours into MOT's cup, knocks the cup over] Title: How to Delete Your Trello Account Trello is a popular project management tool that allows users to organize tasks and projects using boards, lists, and cards. However, there might come a time when you want to delete your Trello account due to various reasons such as no longer needing the service or wanting to declutter your digital footprint. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of deleting your Trello account, providing in-depth explanations, helpful tips, and best practices along the way. ### Before You Begin 1. **Understand what happens when you delete your Trello account:** Once deleted, all your data, including boards, teams, organizations, and personal information associated with your account, cannot be recovered. Make sure you have backed up any critical information before proceeding. 2. **Notify collaborators and team members:** If other people are working with you on shared boards, notify them about your decision to delete your Trello account so they can make alternative arrangements for managing those projects. 3. **Consider transferring ownership:** Instead of permanently deleting your Trello account, consider transferring board ownership to another user if possible. This ensures continued access to important resources without disrupting ongoing work. 4. **Backup essential data:** Export necessary data from your Trello boards by clicking on "Menu" > "More" > "Print and Export." Then choose "Export JSON," which downloads a file containing all card details within the selected board(s). ### Step 1: Log into Your Trello Account Navigate to <https://trello.com/> and enter your email address and password to sign in. It's crucial to log out of any other sessions or devices where you may still be logged in to prevent accidental changes to your account settings. ### Step 2: Access Your Account Settings Once logged in, click on your profile picture at the top right corner of the screen, then select "Settings" from the dropdown menu. This action takes you to the main page displaying your account details, security options, and connected applications. ### Step 3: Navigate to the Account Removal Section On the left sidebar, find and click on "Account Settings." Scroll down until you see the section titled "Account removal." Here, you will find an option labeled "Close your Trello account and personally identifiable information." Click on this button to initiate the deletion process. ### Step 4: Confirm Deletion A popup window will appear asking you to confirm whether you want to close your Trello account. Double-check that you have taken care of everything mentioned earlier (notifying collaborators, backing up data, etc.) since this action is irreversible. Enter your current password in the provided field, check the box indicating that you understand the consequences of closing your account, and finally, click on the red "Yes, Close My Account" button. After completing these steps, you should receive confirmation via email stating that your Trello account has been successfully closed. Remember, once you've deleted your Trello account, there's no going back! So ensure you're making the right decision based on your needs and circumstances.
